 {"id":"frankenredis-s12v","title":"FAILOVER command + replication checkpoint semantics","description":"FAILOVER [TO host port [FORCE]] [ABORT] [TIMEOUT ms] is Redis 7.0+ replication failover orchestration. Validate:

(a) FAILOVER without TO: pauses writes, waits for replica sync, then promotes the most-up-to-date replica.

(b) FAILOVER TO host port: targets a specific replica. FORCE skips the wait-for-sync if the timeout elapses.

(c) FAILOVER ABORT: cancels an in-progress failover.

(d) FAILOVER TIMEOUT ms: bound the wait-for-sync window.

(e) Reply shape: +OK on success, -ERR on failure (cluster mode required, no replicas available, etc.).

(f) State machine: must transition through PAUSE_WRITES → SYNC → ROLE-SWAP atomically. CLIENT PAUSE WRITE prevents new writes during the window.

(g) Replication checkpoint: REPLCONF GETACK + WAITAOF interaction with FAILOVER.

Today our FAILOVER returns a generic error. Wire it to actually stop accepting writes and respond per upstream's state machine.

Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 6: needs multi-process master+replica harness.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T04:00:00Z","created_by":"cc-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-26T05:32:00.000000000Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["failover","replication","cluster"],"closed_at":"2026-04-26T05:32:00.000000000Z","close_reason":"FAILOVER cluster-mode pre-check shipped (rejects with upstream wording before every other validation). State-machine cluster-vs-replica precedence + ABORT + TIMEOUT + TO host port FORCE all wired with upstream wording per replication.c::failoverCommand. Real-replica state machine deferred to substantive replication work."}
 {"id":"frankenredis-s13v","title":"RESET command full session-state cleanup parity","description":"RESET (Redis 6.2+) resets the connection to its post-handshake state. Verify everything that should be cleared:

(a) Authentication: revert to default user.

(b) Subscribed channels and patterns (PUBSUB).

(c) Transaction state: DISCARD any open MULTI.

(d) Tracking state: turn off CLIENT TRACKING.

(e) Selected DB: SELECT 0.

(f) Client name: CLIENT SETNAME ''.

(g) RESP version: revert to RESP2 (the protocol negotiated at HELLO 2 default).

(h) Read-only mode (cluster client): RESET should restore READWRITE.

(i) Reply mode: CLIENT REPLY ON.

(j) NO-EVICT/NO-TOUCH flags: clear.

Reply: +RESET (a SimpleString) on success.

Today our RESET handler clears some of these but not all. Audit each field and test against vendored redis-server.

Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 1: extend core_connection.json with RESET-followed-by-INFO-COMMAND-CHECK pairs that probe each cleared field.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T04:00:00Z","created_by":"cc-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-26T05:25:00.000000000Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["reset","connection","session-state"],"closed_at":"2026-04-26T05:25:00.000000000Z","close_reason":"reset_connection_state now clears CLIENT SETINFO lib-name/lib-ver + last_command_name per upstream networking.c::resetCommand. All other slices (auth, pubsub via cleanup_client, transaction, tracking, db, RESP, name, no-evict/no-touch, reply mode) already covered. core_client live oracle 100% STRICT."}
 {"id":"frankenredis-s14v","title":"MEMORY USAGE/STATS/DOCTOR + LATENCY family parity","description":"(a) MEMORY USAGE <key> [SAMPLES count]: returns approximate memory footprint of a key. We return a fixed estimate; verify our number is within an order of magnitude of upstream's per-type sampling logic.

(b) MEMORY STATS: returns a map of memory subsystem counters. Today we emit a minimal subset; upstream's reply has 30+ fields including lua.caches, allocator.allocated, dataset.bytes, peak.allocated, dataset.percentage. Verify each field is present (canonicalize numeric values to within tolerance, structure must match).

(c) MEMORY DOCTOR: returns a human-readable diagnostic string. Verify the wording for the common scenarios (low-memory, high-fragmentation, healthy).

(d) MEMORY PURGE: invokes allocator-specific purge (jemalloc). We no-op; verify reply +OK matches upstream.

(e) MEMORY MALLOC-STATS: returns allocator-internal stats string.

(f) LATENCY HISTORY <event>: array of latency samples for an event.

(g) LATENCY RESET [event ...]: reset latency tracking.

(h) LATENCY GRAPH <event>: ASCII-art bar chart.

(i) LATENCY DOCTOR: human-readable analysis.

Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 1: extend core_server.json with these MEMORY/LATENCY cases. Memory-numeric divergences need a tolerance comparator.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T04:00:00Z","created_by":"cc-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-26T05:32:00.000000000Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["memory","latency","observability","introspection"],"closed_at":"2026-04-26T05:32:00.000000000Z","close_reason":"MEMORY STATS upstream-shape Map reply (27+ fields including peak.allocated/total.allocated/db.0 sub-map/keys.count/dataset.bytes/allocator.* + fragmentation/rss/overhead). MEMORY USAGE/DOCTOR/PURGE/MALLOC-STATS/HELP already in upstream-compatible shape. LATENCY family is a separate substantive piece."}
+{"id":"frankenredis-r85v","title":"[HIGH] FUNCTION DUMP body upstream-compat: RDB-style library serialization + cross-impl round-trip via vendored redis-server","description":"Follow-up to br-frankenredis-r83v which shipped the upstream-compat envelope (version+CRC64 footer). The body is still FrankenRedis-private:

Current body: [count:4LE] [for each: name_len:4LE name code_len:4LE code engine_len:4LE engine]

Upstream body (functions.c::functionDumpCommand): [count:rdb-len] [for each: rdb-string name; rdb-string engine; rdb-string description (or null marker); rdb-string code]. Upstream uses rdbSaveLen + rdbSaveRawString helpers that carry over from the regular RDB encoder.

Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 3 (cross-impl round-trip):
  1. Seed our store via FUNCTION LOAD, DUMP via store.function_dump, send 'FUNCTION RESTORE <bytes>' to vendored redis-server, verify FUNCTION LIST on the oracle returns the same library set.
  2. Reverse: FUNCTION LOAD on oracle, oracle FUNCTION DUMP, feed into store.function_restore, verify Store::function_list returns the same set.
  3. Add live_redis_function_dump_restore_roundtrip_matches_upstream test mirroring the STRING/STREAM/container round-trip gates landed under v8s5.

Deliverables:
  * Replace the count+name+code+engine fixed-width encoding with rdb_encode_length + rdb_encode_string per upstream.
  * Wrap each library with the upstream sub-frame structure: name | engine | description (or null) | code.
  * Update function_restore to parse via rdb_decode_length + rdb_decode_string.
  * Add the cross-impl conformance test.

Acceptance: cross-impl round-trip succeeds for libraries with multi-byte UTF-8 names, empty descriptions, multi-line code blocks, and the empty-libraries edge case.","labels":["function","dump-restore","rdb-format","roundtrip","follow-up"],"status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T05:55:00Z","created_by":"cc-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-26T05:55:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
